title: uxentlayout
html:
uxentlayout

Add to My Topics
Add selected topic and subtopicsAdd Topic & Subtopics with topic headAdd entire publication
Watch
Download PDF
Save selected topicSave selected topic and subtopicsSave entire publication
Share
Email LinkCopy Link
Feedback
  • Last UpdatedMai 04, 2026
    • Uniface
    • 10.4

The uxentlayout provides two container components for managing Uniface entity data: CollectionLayout and OccurrenceLayout. These widgets work together to create structured, flexible layouts for displaying entity layout collections and individual occurrences, following the Fluent Design System.

The CollectionLayout widget manages a collection-level container that creates placeholder elements for entity layout occurrences, allowing Uniface to dynamically bind and manage individual occurrence widgets. The OccurrenceLayout widget manages the layout and presentation of individual entity layout occurrences and their occurrence field widgets.

Default Logical Widgets

The default logical widget is ux-Layout.

Triggers

None

Keyboard support

The uxentlayout widget is a container and does not support any keyboard navigation by itself.

Layout processing

The uxentlayout widget has been constructed using the uf-layout custom web component built with the Fast Framework, ensuring efficient rendering and integration with Fluent UI.

The resulting DOM structure looks like the following:
<uf-layout class="u-coll-layout">
            <uf-layout class="u-occ-layout">
                       <!-- Direct child content here -->
            <uf-layout>
</uf-layout>

Widget properties

The uxentlayout widget supports the following properties:

CollectionLayout Properties

Table 1. Common Widget Properties
Property Description
class:classname Boolean value that adds or removes the class specified by the classname.
area-slot Explicitly assign widgets to specific slots. The options are:
  • header
  • main (default)
  • footer
appearance Defines the appearance of widget. The available options are:
  • transparent (default): Background, border, and shadow are not visible.
  • outline: Only border lines around the area is visible, no fill inside.
  • card: A bordered box with a filled background, and a drop shadow that creates a raised look.
  • section: A tinted background area to group content together, no border.
  • panel: A bordered box with a subtle layered background, no shadow.
Table 2. Layout-based Properties
Property Description
layout-type-occurrences Controls how child widget occurrences are laid out within the collection and how overflow is handled:
  • horizontal-scroll : Horizontal layout with scroll overflow.
  • horizontal-wrap: Horizontal layout that wraps into multiple lines.
  • vertical-scroll: Vertical layout with scroll overflow
  • vertical-wrap: Vertical layout that wraps into multiple columns.
  • auto (default): Uses the effective layout behavior from the nearest ancestor.
horizontal-align-occurrences Controls how child widget occurrences are positioned along the horizontal direction (reversed in RTL) when not overflowing. If the layout is horizontal, it maps to justify-content and if the layout is vertical, it maps to align-items in CSS. The options are:
  • start: Left align occurrences.
  • center: Center align occurrences.
  • end: Right align occurrences.
  • space-between: Distribute occurrences with equal space between them, first and last at edges. Ignored for vertical layout types.
  • space-around: Distribute occurrences evenly with equal space around them. Ignored for vertical layout types.
  • space-evenly: Distribute occurrences with equal space both between them and at the edges. Ignored for vertical layout types.
  • stretch: Stretch occurrences to fill parent widgets area/container.
    Note: In horizontal layout and vertical layout, stretch behavior is not supported for the following widgets:
    • uxCheckbox
    • uxSwitch
    • uxRadioGroup
    • uxPlainText
  • auto (default): Uses the effective horizontal alignment from the nearest ancestor.
vertical-align-occurrences Controls how child widget occurrences are positioned along the vertical direction when not overflowing. If the layout is vertical, it maps to justify-content and if the layout is horizontal, it maps to align-items in CSS. The options are:
  • start: Align to top.
  • center: Align to center.
  • end: Align to bottom.
  • space-between: Distribute occurrences with equal space between them, first and last at edges. Ignored for horizontal layout types.
  • space-around: Distribute occurrences evenly with equal space around. Ignored for horizontal layout types.
  • space-evenly: Distribute occurrences with equal space both between them and at the edges. Ignored for horizontal layout types.
  • stretch: Stretch occurrences to fill parent widgets area/container.
    Note: In horizontal layout and vertical layout, only uxtextarea supports vertical stretch.
  • auto (default): Uses the effective vertical alignment from the nearest ancestor.
Table 3. Label-based Properties
Property Description
label-text Shows specified text as label. By default, it uses a <span> tag.
label-size Specifies the size and corresponding HTML tag used to render the label text. The available options are:
  • normal (default): Render label text as <span>.
  • small: Render label text as <h3>.
  • medium: Render label text as <h2>.
  • large: Render label text as <h1>.
label-align Specifies how the label text is aligned based on the label position. The options are:
  • start (default): Align to start (left/top).
  • center: Align to center.
  • end: Align to end (right/bottom)
label-position Specifies the position of the label with respect to its element. The options are:
  • above (default): The label is rendered above the element
  • below: The label is rendered below the element.
  • before: The label is rendered before the element.
  • after: The label is rendered after the element.
Note: We provide default density configured with the widget to ensure consistent and appropriate spacing.

OccurrenceLayout Properties

Table 4. Common Widget Properties
Property Description
class:classname Boolean value that adds or removes the class specified by the classname.
appearance-occurrences Defines the appearance of occurrences. The available options are:
  • transparent (default): Background, border, and shadow are not visible.
  • outline: Only border lines around the area is visible, no fill inside.
  • card: A bordered box with a filled background, and a drop shadow that creates a raised look.
  • section: A tinted background area to group content together, no border.
  • panel: A bordered box with a subtle layered background, no shadow.
Table 5. Layout-based Properties
Property Description
layout-type Controls how child widgets (fields or entities) are laid out and how overflow is handled. The available options are:
  • horizontal-scroll : Horizontal layout with scroll overflow.
  • horizontal-wrap: Horizontal layout that wraps into multiple lines.
  • vertical-scroll: Vertical layout with scroll overflow
  • vertical-wrap: Vertical layout that wraps into multiple columns.
  • auto (default): Uses the effective layout behavior from the nearest ancestor.
horizontal-align Controls how child widget (fields or entities) are positioned along the horizontal direction (reversed in RTL) when not overflowing. If the layout is horizontal, it maps to justify-content and if the layout is vertical, it maps to align-items in CSS. The options are:
  • start: Left align child widgets.
  • center: Center align child widgets.
  • end: Right align child widgets.
  • space-between: Distribute child widgets with equal space between them, first and last at edges. Ignored for vertical layout types.
  • space-around: Distribute child widgets evenly with equal space around each widget. Ignored for vertical layout types.
  • space-evenly: Distribute child widgets with equal space both between them and at the edges. Ignored for vertical layout types.
  • stretch: Stretch child widgets to fill parent widgets area/container.
    Note: In horizontal layout and vertical layout, stretch behavior is not supported for the following widgets:
    • uxCheckbox
    • uxSwitch
    • uxRadioGroup
    • uxPlainText
  • auto (default): Uses the effective horizontal alignment from the nearest ancestor.
vertical-align Controls how child widget (fields or entities) are positioned along the vertical direction when not overflowing. If the layout is vertical, it maps to justify-content and if the layout is horizontal, it maps to align-items in CSS. The options are:
  • start: Align to top.
  • center: Align to center.
  • end: Align to bottom.
  • space-between: Distribute child widgets with equal space between them, first and last at edges. Ignored for horizontal layout types.
  • space-around: Distribute child widgets evenly with equal space around each widget. Ignored for horizontal layout types.
  • space-evenly: Distribute child widgets with equal space both between them and at the edges. Ignored for horizontal layout types.
  • stretch: Stretch child widgets to fill parent widgets area/container.
    Note: In horizontal layout and vertical layout, only uxtextarea supports vertical stretch.
  • auto (default): Uses the effective vertical alignment from the nearest ancestor.
Note: We provide default density configured with the widget to ensure consistent and appropriate spacing.

UI blocking

None
Note:

As the widget is not interactive, no UI blocking specific to the widget is applied.

Widget styling

CSS Selector Selected node(s)
.u-coll-layout The Collection widget node
.u-occ-layout The Occurrence widget node

Widget sources

The widget source files, such as JavaScript and CSS files, can be downloaded from the following GitHub repository: https://github.com/RocketSoftware/uniface-ux/tree/main/src/ux/ent_layout.
